Germany/Turkey

German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) Scholarship for International Students in Turkey

Published 10 Jun 20266 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 10 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to DAAD Scholarship for International Students in Turkey

The German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) offers various scholarships to international students in Turkey, providing them with the opportunity to conduct research and academic exchange in Germany. These scholarships are designed to promote academic cooperation between Turkey and Germany, and to support migrant students in pursuing their academic goals. If you are a migrant student in Turkey looking to further your education or conduct research in Germany, the DAAD scholarship is definitely worth considering.

Who Qualifies for the DAAD Scholarship

To be eligible for the DAAD scholarship, you must meet certain criteria, including holding a valid visa for Turkey. The following visa categories are eligible for the DAAD scholarship:

* Student visa (visa type D)

* Research visa (visa type D)

* Work visa (visa type D) with a valid work permit

* Family reunion visa (visa type D) with a valid residence permit

* Refugee status or subsidiary protection with a valid residence permit

* Blue Card EU (visa type D) with a valid work permit

* Other visa categories may also be eligible, please check with the DAAD website for more information

Available DAAD Scholarships

The DAAD offers a range of scholarships for international students in Turkey, each with its own coverage amount, eligibility criteria, and deadline. Here are five real scholarships offered by the DAAD:

Development-Related Postgraduate Courses (EPOS)

The EPOS scholarship covers a monthly stipend of €750, plus tuition fees and travel costs, and is eligible for students pursuing a master's or PhD degree in a development-related field. The deadline for application is usually in July or August, and more information can be found on the official DAAD website at https://www.daad.de.

Helmut-Schmidt-Programme (HSP)

The HSP scholarship covers a monthly stipend of €750, plus tuition fees and travel costs, and is eligible for students pursuing a master's degree in a social science or economics field. The deadline for application is usually in July or August, and more information can be found on the official DAAD website at https://www.daad.de.

Graduate School Scholarship Programme (GSSP)

The GSSP scholarship covers a monthly stipend of €1,200, plus tuition fees and travel costs, and is eligible for students pursuing a PhD degree in a STEM field. The deadline for application is usually in January or February, and more information can be found on the official DAAD website at https://www.daad.de.

Research Grants (DGSS)

The DGSS scholarship covers a monthly stipend of €1,200, plus travel costs, and is eligible for students pursuing a research project in a STEM field. The deadline for application is usually in January or February, and more information can be found on the official DAAD website at https://www.daad.de.

Short-Term Research Grant (STRG)

The STRG scholarship covers a monthly stipend of €1,200, plus travel costs, and is eligible for students pursuing a short-term research project in a STEM field. The deadline for application is usually in January or February, and more information can be found on the official DAAD website at https://www.daad.de.

Application Steps

To apply for a DAAD scholarship, follow these steps:

  • Check your eligibility for the scholarship by reviewing the eligibility criteria on the DAAD website.

  • Choose the scholarship you want to apply for and review the application requirements.

  • Gather all the required documents, including your passport, visa, and academic transcripts.

  • Fill out the online application form on the DAAD website.

  • Upload all the required documents to the online application portal.

  • Submit your application and pay the application fee (if required).

  • Wait for the application to be processed and reviewed by the DAAD.

  • Receive an email notification of the application result, and follow the instructions provided if you are selected for the scholarship.

Documents Checklist

Here is a list of documents you may need to submit with your application:

* Passport

* Visa

* Academic transcripts

* Diploma or certificate

* Language proficiency certificate (if required)

* Research proposal (if required)

* Letter of recommendation (if required)

* Proof of financial support (if required)

Frequently Asked Questions

Here are some frequently asked questions about the DAAD scholarship:

  • What is the deadline for applying for the DAAD scholarship?

The deadline for applying for the DAAD scholarship varies depending on the scholarship and the country of origin, but it is usually between January and August.

  • Can I apply for the DAAD scholarship if I am already studying in Germany?

Yes, you can apply for the DAAD scholarship even if you are already studying in Germany, but you must meet the eligibility criteria and follow the application procedure.

  • Do I need to speak German to apply for the DAAD scholarship?

No, you do not need to speak German to apply for the DAAD scholarship, but you may need to provide proof of language proficiency in English or German, depending on the scholarship.

  • Can I apply for the DAAD scholarship if I am a migrant with a refugee status?

Yes, you can apply for the DAAD scholarship if you are a migrant with a refugee status, but you must meet the eligibility criteria and follow the application procedure.

  • How long does it take to process the application?

The application process usually takes several months, and you will be notified of the result by email.

  • Can I apply for the DAAD scholarship if I am already receiving a scholarship from another organization?

It depends on the scholarship and the organization, but you may be able to apply for the DAAD scholarship if you are already receiving a scholarship from another organization.

  • Do I need to have a guarantor to apply for the DAAD scholarship?

No, you do not need to have a guarantor to apply for the DAAD scholarship, but you may need to provide proof of financial support.

  • Can I apply for the DAAD scholarship if I am a citizen of a country other than Turkey?

Yes, you can apply for the DAAD scholarship if you are a citizen of a country other than Turkey, but you must meet the eligibility criteria and follow the application procedure.

External Resources

For more information about the DAAD scholarship and the application procedure, you can visit the official DAAD website at https://www.daad.de, or the official website of the German Embassy in Turkey at https://turkije.diplo.de.

Conclusion

The DAAD scholarship is a great opportunity for migrant students in Turkey to pursue their academic goals in Germany. With various scholarships available, you can choose the one that best fits your needs and interests. For more information and tips on how to apply for the DAAD scholarship, please visit our website at https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.
